subject: math
prompt: A sample of a certain isotope takes 31 years to decay to half its original mass. The function $s(t)=120(0.5)^{\frac{t}{31}}$ gives the approximate mass of this isotope, in grams, that remains $t$ years after a 120 gram sample starts to decay. Which statement is the best interpretation of $s(62)= 30$ in this context?
questionText: A sample of a certain isotope takes 31 years to decay to half its original mass. The function $s(t)=120(0.5)^{\frac{t}{31}}$ gives the approximate mass of this isotope, in grams, that remains $t$ years after a 120 gram sample starts to decay. Which statement is the best interpretation of $s(62)= 30$ in this context?

choices.text: Approximately 30 grams of the sample remains 62 years after the sample starts to decay.
The mass of the sample has decreased by approximately 30 grams 62 years after the sample starts to decay.
The mass of the sample has decreased by approximately 62 grams 30 years after the sample starts to decay.
Approximately 62 grams of the sample remains 30 years after the sample starts to decay.

subject: math
prompt: To investigate the effect of vitamin D supplementation on the concentration levels of adults, a researcher selected 180 adults at random from a wellness clinic to participate in a study. The researcher first measured the concentration level of each participant. Each participant was then randomly assigned to take a vitamin D supplement or a placebo each day for 8 weeks. At the end of the 8 weeks, the researcher measured the concentration levels of all participants again and found that the vitamin D supplement caused statistically significant improved concentration levels for the adults at the wellness clinic. What feature of this study allowed the researcher to conclude that the vitamin D supplement caused the improved concentration levels?
questionText: To investigate the effect of vitamin D supplementation on the concentration levels of adults, a researcher selected 180 adults at random from a wellness clinic to participate in a study. The researcher first measured the concentration level of each participant. Each participant was then randomly assigned to take a vitamin D supplement or a placebo each day for 8 weeks. At the end of the 8 weeks, the researcher measured the concentration levels of all participants again and found that the vitamin D supplement caused statistically significant improved concentration levels for the adults at the wellness clinic. What feature of this study allowed the researcher to conclude that the vitamin D supplement caused the improved concentration levels?

choices.text: There were more than 100 participants in the study.
The participants were selected at random from the wellness clinic.
The concentration level of each participant was measured again at the end of 8 weeks.
Each participant was randomly assigned to take a vitamin D supplement or a placebo.

prompt: Right rectangular prism $M$ is similar to right rectangular prism $N$. The surface area of right rectangular prism $M$ is 72 square centimeters ($\text{cm}^2$), and the surface area of right rectangular prism $N$ is 648 $\text{cm}^2$. The volume of right rectangular prism $N$ is 1,350 cubic centimeters ($\text{cm}^3$). What is the sum of the volumes, in $\text{cm}^3$, of right rectangular prism $M$ and right rectangular prism $N$?
questionText: Right rectangular prism $M$ is similar to right rectangular prism $N$. The surface area of right rectangular prism $M$ is 72 square centimeters ($\text{cm}^2$), and the surface area of right rectangular prism $N$ is 648 $\text{cm}^2$. The volume of right rectangular prism $N$ is 1,350 cubic centimeters ($\text{cm}^3$). What is the sum of the volumes, in $\text{cm}^3$, of right rectangular prism $M$ and right rectangular prism $N$?

subject: math
prompt: In triangle $PQR,$ the measure of angle $Q$ is $90^\circ$ and $\overline{QS}$ is an altitude of the triangle. The length of $\overline{PQ}$ is 20 and the length of $\overline{PR}$ is 9 greater than the length of $\overline{PQ}$. What is the value of $\frac{QR}{QS}$?
questionText: In triangle $PQR,$ the measure of angle $Q$ is $90^\circ$ and $\overline{QS}$ is an altitude of the triangle. The length of $\overline{PQ}$ is 20 and the length of $\overline{PR}$ is 9 greater than the length of $\overline{PQ}$. What is the value of $\frac{QR}{QS}$?

prompt: A smoothie recipe uses 4 cups of milk and 2 bananas and has 1,158 milligrams (mg) of calcium. There are 286 mg of calcium in 1 cup of milk. How much calcium, in mg, is in 1 banana?
